mainEntity.name: Can I implement schema for AI search myself, or do I really need to hire a specialist?

acceptedAnswer.text:
You can absolutely implement schema yourself, especially if you’re comfortable editing site templates or using a CMS.
The main risks are using the wrong types, mismatching visible content, or introducing technical errors.

Key factors:
- DIY works well for basic Organization, LocalBusiness, Article, and FAQPage schema.
- Schema generators and plugins reduce syntax mistakes but still require careful review.
- Validation tools are essential to catch errors before and after deployment.
- Complex cases (large catalogs, custom properties, many cities) benefit from expert guidance.

Practically, start small: hand-craft a few JSON-LD blocks, validate them, and learn the patterns before scaling.
If you run into recurring issues or need to model nuanced services across cities like Los Angeles and Denver, consider a consult or audit.
DIY plus occasional expert oversight can be a cost-effective middle ground.

short_answer:"Yes, you can DIY schema for AI search if you’re willing to learn JSON-LD basics and use validators. Start with simple types on a few key pages, then bring in expert help if you hit complex multi-location or catalog challenges."
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes, you can DIY schema for AI search if you’re willing to learn JSON-LD basics and use validators. Start with simple types on a few key pages, then bring in expert help if you hit complex multi-location or catalog challenges.
